slug: 王尔德因为太聪明被关进监狱
title: 王尔德因为太聪明被关进监狱

outline.concept: Wilde's epigrams weren't decoration — they were attacks on Victorian hierarchy. Each witty inversion ('I can resist everything except temptation') exposed a social lie, which made powerful people want him gone long before any legal charge.
outline.query: 说真话得罪人 机智反讽 社交场合 说话太直接 树敌

outline.concept: Wilde sued the Marquess of Queensberry for libel, believing his courtroom eloquence could outperform evidence. But legal proceedings reward consistency over cleverness — a witty contradiction that delights a salon destroys credibility under cross-examination.
outline.query: 自信过头 反击失败 以为能赢 辩论输了 逞强

outline.concept: The prosecution weaponized Wilde's own wit against him. His epigrams and aesthetic declarations — meant as paradox — were read literally as confessions of moral corruption. Cleverness that relies on irony becomes self-incriminating when the audience refuses the ironic frame.
outline.query: 聪明反被聪明误 话多必失 解释更糟 被误解 被断章取义

outline.concept: Victorian England could tolerate private transgression but not public defiance. Wilde's real offense was seeing through the system's hypocrisy and saying so while also violating its rules — the combination of insight and visibility is what the system punishes hardest.
outline.query: 看穿规则 被针对 枪打出头鸟 太高调 明知还做
